parsed_review.summary: CP00-415 (RP13.P08.M06.S02-RP13.P09.M05.S01, Risk C, 150 units) is a descriptor-only payments-core closeout pack whose claims are fully supported by the implementation, tests, and validation evidence. Pack metadata is identical across the registry binding (registry.js:392), contract current_pack, manifest, and review baseline; the deliverable distribution (hermes_evidence 47 / claude_review 17 / implementation 64 / test 8 / security_audit 10 / ui 4), phase counts (P08:89 + P09:61), micro-phase row counts, and micro-title counts each independently reconcile to 150 and match the prompt. The pack does NOT overclaim production readiness: command-evidence.json and manifest.json both set production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false with status implemented_pending_review; the production_ready_candidate=true value is solely the local coverage+slice validity flag, not a runtime trust claim. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: a sweep of packages/payments/src found no fs/network/child_process/DB/process.env usage, all imports are internal, and every descriptor/case-set/validator returns Object.freeze(...) static data; the no_write_attestation and manifest implementation_summary carry the full set of false runtime flags (writes_product_state, loads_real_fixture_data, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, executes_command_runtime, etc.). The authority boundary is preserved everywhere: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, read_only=true with tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, and enterprise trust not claimed from local validation — all asserted in model.test.js (1575-1633) and enforced via deep-equality in validate-rp13-payments-core-contract.mjs (322-324, 1310-1324). Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic evidence-packet/review-receipt labels (H13/C13), not product runtime (no_hermes_runtime_receipt=true). CP178+ review hardening is intact: forbidden_review_evidence enumerates auth-failure, zero-byte, malformed-JSON, fenced-JSON, tool-call-shaped, disabled-tools, missing-reference, claude-final-approval, source-mutation, and enterprise-trust blockers; readiness-report shows a passing auth preflight with 1434-byte stdout; validation-report confirms read_tools_used mode, forbidden_tools_present=false, and summary_only_review_allowed=false for this Risk C pack. The command-evidence records all required validation commands (rp13:payments-core:validate, npm test, npm run validate, git diff --check, etc.) at exit_code 0. One minor, non-blocking metadata observation: the pack's own untracked evidence dir is double-listed in changed-file-scope.json. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. Per the authority boundary, this PASS is an independent read-only reviewer recommendation only; final closeout still requires the downstream adjudication/inspection artifacts and human final approval.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-lists pack evidence dir as both implementation and unrelated
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-415/ (the pack's own untracked manifest + command-evidence dir) and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 131-132) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 137-138). These two classifications are semantically opposed (pack-implementation vs. unrelated-but-preserved). The substantive review is unaffected because active_pack_implementation_refs correctly enumerates the 8 reviewed code/contract/test/script files, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, and the dual listing is plausibly by design (preserve-list is a superset of everything dirty outside active code refs).
findings.recommendation: For metadata clarity, classify the pack's own evidence directory under a single category (pack-implementation/required-untracked) rather than also tagging it unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, or document that the preserve list intentionally overlaps the implementation list. No code or boundary change required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; non-blocking; does not affect production_ready, no-write, or authority-boundary conclusions. Likely intentional scope-tool behavior. No action required for closeout.
